findings.locus: §7 Cross-References + §2.2 table + §5.2 — subject-name for the forthcoming ingestion record
findings.claim: The same forthcoming ingestion artifact is named non-identically across the loci that cite it: §2.2 table and §5.2 refer to 'the forthcoming ingestion decision record', while §2.2 prose and §7 refer to 'the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record'. A reader resolving the record's own cross-references cannot verify these denote one artifact or two (a general ingestion record vs a file-driven-specific one); the subject-name of the forthcoming record is not stable across the document, so the pointer-resolution obligation the downstream record must satisfy at landing is under-specified.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 table + §5.2 ('the forthcoming ingestion decision record') in conflict with ADR-008 §2.2 Environment/ingestion prose + §7 ('the forthcoming file-driven ingestion decision record')

findings.authority_locus: Ruling G (ADR-008 run-025/026 operator rulings) + author-decision-record SKILL body-integrity forward-reference rule (subject-name only)

findings.locus: §2.2 Environment row 'Realization routes to' cell vs the four sibling rows
findings.claim: The 'Realization routes to' column names a destination record or port in every other row (the graph-schema ruling; the forthcoming ingestion decision record + ingestion port; the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design + detection-promotion service design). The Environment row's cell instead carries a build-status/substantiation label ('Form built (DDR-004); constants contested/unvalidated (n=0)'), conflating the column's stated function (where realization routes) with a status claim; the column contract is not honored uniformly across the table's own rows.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 table Environment 'Realization routes to' cell (status label) vs the other four rows' 'Realization routes to' cells (destination records/ports) — intra-table column-contract inconsistency

findings.authority_locus: Ruling D (ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings) — Environment control routes to DDR-004 (capture-time aging derivation §1/§3 + §6 constants governance); status relabel is a separate cell

findings.locus: §1 Context — 'the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design is blocked on this record' / 'Authority that multiple accepted records depend on does not yet exist' vs metadata Status: PROPOSED
findings.claim: §1 states that downstream work is blocked on this record and that accepted records depend on the authority it homes, framing the ADR as the load-bearing dependency the corpus is waiting on. The record's own metadata carries Status: PROPOSED (v0.1.0). A PROPOSED, unratified record has not yet decided anything the corpus can bind to; the authority it purports to home is not established until acceptance, so the downstream blockage is discharged only pending this record's own acceptance. The Context asserts a present-standing dependency relationship the record's status has not yet earned.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 metadata (Status: PROPOSED) vs §1 Context ('the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design is blocked on this record'; 'Authority that multiple accepted records depend on does not yet exist')

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL, 'Tense is earned by enforcement' / 'Status claims are earned by the trail' body-integrity rules + ADR template §1 Context (forcing-function convention)

findings.locus: §2.2 Promotion row 'Per-write (EA)' / §2.3 'the same per-write ... control class' vs the built PromotionDecision batch form
findings.claim: §2.2 labels the Promotion control 'Per-write (EA)' and §2.3 characterizes un-promotion as 'the same per-write, EA-gated, human-accountable control class' — a label the record uses deliberately in contrast to the Environment 'per-policy' class. The built instance the ADR cites as this control's realization (DDR-002 §2.4/§5) is batch-capable: a single PromotionDecision may decide multiple candidates via a 1:many DECIDED_ON edge with a per-candidate verdict on each edge. A single human decision act deciding N candidate materializations is not literally per-materialized-write in the sense §2.2 uses 'per-write' for ingestion (one human confirmation per captured representation); the accountability granularity of the cited built instance is per-decision-act-over-a-batch, which the 'per-write' label does not disclose.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 Promotion 'Per-write (EA)' / §2.3 'same per-write ... control class' vs DDR-002 §2.4 (PromotionDecision DECIDED_ON → CandidatePromotion, 1:many batch; one decision decides multiple candidates, per-candidate verdict on each edge)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Governing principle (accountability control type per class; human accountability at the decision act) + DDR-002 §2.4 (PromotionDecision 1:many batch, per-candidate verdict)
findings.resolution_note: conformed to DDR-002 §2.4 (PromotionDecision DECIDED_ON 1:many batch, per-candidate verdict on each edge) + ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Governing principle (human accountability at the decision act)
